project.name: TrustAnchor
project.domain: Anti-counterfeit certificate issuance and verification web application
confirmedContext: Used by education institutions and government agencies
Combines digital signatures, Autokey Cipher payload transformation, double encryption, audit logging, QR lookup, and instant verification
Runs on Next.js App Router with TypeScript
Public verification exposes safe public certificate fields and proof status
assumptionsToValidate: The intaglio register direction feels credible for education and public-sector users
A single warm security accent provides enough status distinction with text and shape support
The locked hero composition should remain a serial register plate unless a later user-approved redesign changes it

designEvidenceSummary.currentFrontendShape: Next.js App Router UI with public home, login, verification result, print artifact, and admin pages

discardedVisualDna: sunlit forensic registry light table metaphor
porcelain, sky, mint, marigold, coral, and ultraviolet palette
daylight sweep, paper lift, color-tab feedback, and status stamp settlement
orbit rings, scanner circles, and telemetry lane language
generic SaaS admin shell and equal card grid rhythm
newDirection: banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register anchor
engraved monochrome palette with graphite ink, ivory paper, and one warm spot security accent
serial-compare flip motion for verification state changes
engraved type contrast with heavy condensed display and OCR-style proof values
compact micro-ledger rail composition instead of card grids or admin chrome

designPhilosophy: TrustAnchor should make authenticity feel like a serial proof comparison against an institutional register, not a generic certificate dashboard.
motionPaletteDecision: Use a banknote intaglio proof register anchor with graphite ink, ivory paper, one warm security accent, serial-compare flip motion, and engraved/OCR type contrast.

locked: zero-based redesign from the prior visual direction
banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register anchor
engraved monochrome plus one warm security accent direction
token continuity classifications approved by the user
flexibleUntilValidated: exact font files or system stacks
designFlexibilityPolicy.continuityPolicy: Prior visual direction is blocked unless the user explicitly approves revival. Exact primitives remain flexible until implementation and accessibility validation.

conceptualAnchor.name: Banknote Intaglio Plate Proof and Serial-Number Register
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: Banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: A banknote plate proof sheet paired with a serial-number register, where engraved line weight, paper tone, overprint marks, serial rows, and proof comparison make authenticity legible.
conceptualAnchor.rationale: TrustAnchor verifies whether a certificate record is authentic and unchanged, so serial proof comparison and register logic fit the product better than generic credential dashboards.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: Serial-compare flip motion reveals verified, revoked, or invalid proof state changes while reduced motion swaps to instant state replacement.
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: Heavy condensed engraved display roles contrast with readable institutional body copy and OCR-style monospace proof values using tabular numbers and slashed zero where available.

derivedTokenLogic.typography: Newly introduced engraved display contrast plus OCR-style proof values; not continuity from the blocked condensed/sans/mono trio.
derivedTokenLogic.density: Medium-high register density with public verification first on mobile.
derivedTokenLogic.colorBehavior: Anchor-derived graphite ink, neutral low-chroma ivory paper, and one warm intaglio spot color for security emphasis while avoiding cream-heavy or sepia dominance.
derivedTokenLogic.morphology: Low-radius register rails, engraved line separators, aligned serial fields, proof rows, and struck entries for revoked or invalid states.
derivedTokenLogic.motion: Anchor-derived serial-compare flip and manifest handoff behavior.
derivedTokenLogic.responsiveComposition: Mobile single serial register, tablet paired verification and proof register, desktop compact ledger rails.

candidateEntries: credential SaaS default typography and gradient hero

typographyClusters.code: credential SaaS default: Plus Jakarta Sans, Inter, or Manrope display paired with JetBrains Mono or Geist Mono proof snippets, large rounded hero type, and tiny uppercase metadata
typographyClusters.reason: Observed credential products commonly use modern rounded sans typography with monospace hashes to signal technical trust.
typographyClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it makes TrustAnchor read as interchangeable credential SaaS.

typographyClusters.code: public-sector verifier default: Poppins plus Source Sans Pro or IBM Plex Sans, plain institutional headings, and minimal proof-code differentiation
typographyClusters.reason: Public verification portals often choose plain civic sans stacks that prioritize neutrality over proof identity.
typographyClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it flattens the product into a portal.

typographyClusters.code: security proof console default: condensed technical headings plus civic sans body plus monospace proof values
typographyClusters.reason: Security-flavored UIs often combine condensed labels, sans copy, and mono hashes for instant technical credibility.
typographyClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this as derivation; the existing TrustAnchor trio belongs to this autopilot cluster and remains blocked.

paletteClusters.code: navy, white, cyan, and orange institutional verifier palette with government-portal contrast
paletteClusters.reason: Certificate verification portals often use navy authority with blue/orange action accents.
paletteClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it repeats public-sector portal defaults.

paletteClusters.code: slate, violet, emerald, and white SaaS trust gradient palette
paletteClusters.reason: Credential SaaS sites often mix violet startup energy with emerald verification cues.
paletteClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it is too close to startup trust marketing.

paletteClusters.code: porcelain, sky inspection light, mint verification, marigold audit, coral caution, and ultraviolet hidden-layer palette
paletteClusters.reason: This is the previous TrustAnchor light-table palette.
paletteClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it is explicitly blocklisted history.

layoutClusters.code: hero with verification card beside marketing claims and a feature grid below
layoutClusters.reason: Credential products often place a sample verifier beside value proposition copy.
layoutClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it is marketing shell rhythm.

layoutClusters.code: left sidebar, top utility bar, KPI card row, and table-first admin dashboard
layoutClusters.reason: Admin products often default to this layout because it is easy to scaffold.
layoutClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this as the visual identity; TrustAnchor needs register rails instead.

layoutClusters.code: centered dropzone or certificate upload panel with recognized institution logo strip
layoutClusters.reason: Certificate verification portals often foreground upload or file-drop patterns.
layoutClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; TrustAnchor verifies code and QR-backed records, not a generic upload portal.

motionClusters.code: fade-up page reveal, hover scale, and shimmer button motion
motionClusters.reason: Modern SaaS pages often use these low-risk animations.
motionClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this as the signature; it says nothing about proof comparison.

motionClusters.code: scanner rings, orbit motion, and telemetry pulses
motionClusters.reason: Security dashboards often use scanning metaphors to imply detection.
motionClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it revives blocked observatory and scanner language.

motionClusters.code: daylight sweep, paper lift, color-tab feedback, and stamp settlement
motionClusters.reason: This is the previous TrustAnchor light-table motion signature.
motionClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; it is explicitly blocklisted history.

imageryClusters.code: shield, check, badge, QR, and certificate mockup icon collage
imageryClusters.reason: Trust and credential apps often assemble these symbols as fast recognition.
imageryClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this as decoration; imagery must serve register or serial proof work.

imageryClusters.code: abstract aurora, glass card, and blockchain network background
imageryClusters.reason: Cryptographic credential products often borrow Web3 atmospherics.
imageryClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this; TrustAnchor should feel like a proof register, not a Web3 landing page.

imageryClusters.code: institution logo strip as authority proof
imageryClusters.reason: Education credential products often use partner logos to build trust.
imageryClusters.rejectionNote: I will not ship this until real approved institution assets exist.

uncomfortableCombination.discomfortReason: It risks feeling like a slow archive instead of a verification tool.
uncomfortableCombination.productLogicJustification: Certificate provenance can be understood as accession into a permanent record. A slower folio rhythm could work for audit review, but it is not the chosen public verification direction.

anchorCandidates.selectedAnchorReference: Banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register

candidates.anchorReference: Banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register
candidates.jobFit: Certificates need anti-counterfeit proof, and serial comparison maps directly to verification codes and hashes.
candidates.hierarchyImplication: Serial proof and security features lead the page.
candidates.densityImplication: High micro-ledger density is acceptable when proof values remain readable.
candidates.typeImplication: Heavy engraved display roles contrast with OCR-style tabular proof values.
candidates.motionImplication: Serial compare flip reveals state changes.
candidates.whatItRulesOut: Soft SaaS cards, light-table sweeps, and generic badge visuals.

categoryCodeOverlap.reason: Proof values still use a monospace role, so the design must avoid the old condensed/sans/mono trio and declare typography newly introduced.

candidates.jobFit: TrustAnchor stores proof material and audit history as a sealed custody trail.
candidates.hierarchyImplication: Sealed packet, handler, timestamp, and break condition drive hierarchy.
candidates.densityImplication: Medium manifest density with strong seal strips.
candidates.typeImplication: Humanist claims, stencil-like seal numerals, and tabular audit rows.
candidates.motionImplication: Seal band locks and revocation tears through the seal.

categoryCodeOverlap.reason: Seal state must replace generic verified badges.

candidates.anchorReference: Passport visa page with MRZ and entry stamps
candidates.jobFit: Public verification resembles admitting or denying a document claim.
candidates.hierarchyImplication: Identity claim, issuer stamp, validity window, and MRZ proof form the hierarchy.
candidates.typeImplication: Official document type, OCR-B-like MRZ, and stamp labels.
candidates.stateLanguage: admitted, denied, expired, revoked, secondary review

categoryCodeOverlap.reason: It could become generic document cosplay if not tied to proof state.

candidates.anchorReference: Archival accession ledger and catalog card system
candidates.jobFit: Issuance is an immutable accession into institutional record.
candidates.hierarchyImplication: Accession number, collection, provenance note, and withdrawal status lead.
candidates.typeImplication: Editorial serif headings, tabular sans metadata, and mono hashes only where needed.
candidates.motionImplication: Catalog card slides into register and revocation stamps across an index line.

categoryCodeOverlap.reason: It needs stronger type contrast than a plain civic portal.

candidates.anchorReference: ISO cargo container high-security seal manifest
candidates.jobFit: Each certificate is a sealed shipment of claims whose seal number must match the manifest.
candidates.hierarchyImplication: Seal number, manifest, route, and receiving verifier lead.
candidates.typeImplication: Industrial sans, large seal numerals, and compact tabular event rows.
candidates.stateLanguage: sealed, in transit, received, seal mismatch, released, quarantined
candidates.motionImplication: Manifest handoff, seal-number latch, and mismatch quarantine slide.
candidates.whatItRulesOut: Academic certificate prettiness and generic trust badges.

categoryCodeOverlap.reason: Manifest rhythm must replace KPI/table chrome.

conceptualAnchor.visualRiskBudget: Engraved density is allowed only when proof values, forms, and state text remain readable.
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: Serial flip and manifest handoff are allowed for state transitions. Reduced motion must replace them with instant state changes.

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: Banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register
derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Palette derives from intaglio plate ink and paper: graphite ink, neutral low-chroma ivory stock, neutral shadows and focus treatment, and one warm spot color for security accent. The paper cue must not become cream-heavy or sepia-dominant.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: Spacing derives from compact serial registers and micro-ledger rails, with tight row rhythm and enough clearance for proof strings.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: Typography is newly introduced: engraved display contrast and OCR-style proof values are fresh choices, not continuity from the blocked prior font trio and not a literal physical derivation. Implementation fallback chains must exclude Arial Narrow, Bahnschrift, DIN Condensed, Segoe UI, Noto Sans, SFMono-Regular, and Consolas.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: Motion derives from serial comparison and manifest handoff: verification flips between compared serial states, and proof detail moves into the register after lookup.
derivedTokenLogic.colorSpace: OKLCH preferred for new CSS primitives when browser support and current project patterns allow it.
derivedTokenLogic.spatialBaseUnit: Compact 6px or 8px micro-ledger base, to be selected during implementation after readability testing.
derivedTokenLogic.typeScaleMethod: Role-based scale with heavy condensed display, readable body, compact labels, and OCR-style monospace proof values.
derivedTokenLogic.motionBudget: 150-300ms for serial flips and control feedback, 300-500ms for manifest handoff, transform/opacity first, reduced-motion fallback required.
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: Every visual token must support serial proof comparison, register reading, or anti-counterfeit state clarity.

libraryDecisions.name: qrcode-generator
libraryDecisions.purpose: Render scannable verification QR matrices as SVG without a binary runtime dependency
libraryDecisions.risk: The Docker node_modules volume must be refreshed after package changes
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: Keep verification code text visible and defer QR rendering until dependency install succeeds

libraryDecisions.name: No new animation, icon, canvas, or UI primitive library for this documentation step
libraryDecisions.purpose: The approved bootstrap direction can be planned before implementation dependency research
libraryDecisions.risk: Future implementation must research official docs before adding UI or motion packages
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: Native CSS transforms, opacity, and existing React components

visualDirection.name: Engraved serial proof register
distinctiveMoves: Public verification centers a serial compare register instead of a marketing card
Verification result changes use serial-compare flip motion
Proof strings read as OCR-style register values with tabular numbers and slashed zero where available
Admin lists become compact micro-ledger rails rather than KPI cards plus generic tables
Invalid and revoked states use mismatch, overprint, or struck-register language
visualDirection.heroComposition: Serial register plate: a public lookup panel paired with an abstract plate-proof register showing presented serial, registry serial, microprint lines, and a compact proof rail.

assetRoles.primary: Code-native serial register plate used to explain proof comparison and anti-counterfeit registration.
forbidden: generic shield or check badge collage

colorTruth.baseBehavior: engraved monochrome with one warm security accent

mutationRules.mobile: Verification input and result summary appear first; serial rails collapse into a single register stack.
mutationRules.tablet: Verification and proof register pair into two readable bands; admin filters move above ledger rows.
mutationRules.desktop: Compact register columns and proof rails expose more context without defaulting to admin chrome.

forbiddenPatterns: sidebar-first admin chrome as the primary visual system
desktop density that obscures proof strings

motionSystem.dependencyDecision: No new UI dependency for this documentation step; future implementation dependency choices require official docs research.
motionSystem.approach: Serial-compare flip, manifest handoff, struck-register invalidation, transform and opacity first
signatureMoves: serial-compare flip motion for verify state change
manifest handoff motion for proof detail reveal

identityRules: Use micro-ledger rails for repeated proof and audit data
Use engraved line separators only when they organize reading
Use OCR-style proof values with tabular numbers and slashed zero where available
Use low-radius register controls instead of pill-heavy SaaS controls
Status marks must include readable text and may not rely on color alone

accessibilityPolicy.proofValueHandling: Long hashes and verification codes must wrap, scroll, or truncate with access to the full value.

genericityRisks: reviving the light-table palette under new labels
requiredProductSignals: engraved display and OCR proof type contrast
aiSafeUiAudit.renameTestRule: If the UI can be renamed to a generic credential SaaS product without changing composition, palette, typography, and motion, revise before implementation is complete.

beforeUiImplementation: Preserve the locked serial register plate hero composition and asset roles
Research official docs before adding any UI, motion, icon, canvas, or primitive dependency
Confirm exact type stack and fallbacks
Confirm contrast for graphite, ivory, and warm accent roles
implementationBoundaries: Preserve route behavior and API contracts
Do not alter cryptographic proof behavior as part of visual work
Keep proof values readable before adding density
Do not copy external reference visuals

designExecutionHandoff.nextStep: Validate the implemented intaglio register direction across desktop and mobile.
designExecutionHandoff.primaryAnchor: Banknote intaglio plate proof and serial-number register
designExecutionHandoff.approvedPaletteDirection: engraved monochrome plus single warm security accent

reviewRubric.distinctiveness: The UI must read as TrustAnchor's engraved serial proof register, not a generic credential dashboard.
reviewRubric.contractFidelity: Composition, type, palette, and motion must trace to serial comparison and intaglio register logic.
reviewRubric.hierarchy: Public verification must be the clearest first action.
reviewRubric.responsiveRecomposition: Mobile, tablet, and desktop must change grouping and priority.
reviewRubric.motionDiscipline: Motion must explain proof comparison or state transition and stop under reduced motion.
reviewRubric.accessibility: Focus, contrast, target size, form errors, status text, and proof-value readability must remain intact.

validStyleContext: external references used only for mechanics and quality bars
taintedContext: external website layouts, palettes, component skins, and brand posture
prior-chat visual memory not repeated in this task

forbiddenPatterns: sunlit forensic registry light table continuity
porcelain, sky, mint, marigold, coral, and ultraviolet palette
daylight sweep, paper lift, color-tab feedback, and stamp-settle motion
observatory signal rings and scanner circles
purple-only or dark control-room gradient hero shells
proof strings styled like marketing copy
decorative linework or microprint wallpaper without product function

metadata.freshnessRule: Research dossier is stale when researchVerifiedAt is null or older than freshnessWindowDays. User-explicit redesign requests bypass freshness and force fresh research regardless of age.

previousPalettes.summary: Porcelain paper, graphite ink, sky inspection light, mint verification stamps, marigold audit tabs, coral caution marks, and ultraviolet hidden layers.

previousMotionSignatures.summary: Daylight sweep, paper lift, color-tab feedback, and status stamp settlement.
Orbit rings, scanner circles, and telemetry lane motion.

previousTypographyChoices.summary: Condensed inspection headings, civic sans body copy, compact uppercase labels, and monospaced microprint proof values.

productReading.productTypeAndCoreVerb: TrustAnchor is an anti-counterfeit certificate issuance and verification web application; the core verb is seal and verify public proof.
productReading.highestStakesMoments: The highest-stakes moments are public verification by code or QR, institution issuance or revocation, and audit review.
productReading.dominantDataShapes: The dominant screen data shapes are documents, hashes, QR links, audit ledgers, admin lists, and forms.
productReading.latencyProfile: The latency profile is soft real-time for verification and batch or admin-paced for issuance and audit review.
productReading.visibleFailureModes: The UI must visibly absorb not found, revoked, stale, proof invalid, permission, rate-limit, loading, partial, and validation error states.
productReading.contextOfUse: The context is mixed: glance-and-go for public verification and sustained focus for admin issuance and audit tasks.
productReading.knownConstraints: Known constraints are Next.js App Router, WCAG 2.2 AA, Docker delivery, public-safe fields only, CSRF protection, Redis rate limiting, and no secret exposure.

referenceIntake.borrowed: Direct certificate verification and view/check/verify task clarity.
referenceIntake.notBorrowed: Navy-orange palette, Poppins and Source Sans Pro surface, dropzone composition, and partner-logo trust strip.

referenceIntake.borrowed: Public URL and QR permanence plus proof-status framing.
referenceIntake.notBorrowed: Aurora gradient, Web3 atmosphere, Manrope/Inter/Geist Mono stack, and blockchain hero posture.

referenceIntake.borrowed: Issue, verify, and share lifecycle with fast public confidence.
referenceIntake.notBorrowed: Purple and emerald SaaS gradients, rounded card stack, Plus Jakarta Sans and JetBrains Mono skin.

referenceIntake.borrowed: Selective disclosure and privacy framing for credential proof.
referenceIntake.notBorrowed: IBM corporate page rhythm, palette, and component treatment.

referenceIntake.borrowed: Issuer, holder, verifier, registry roles and verification-status caution.

nextValidationAction: Validate the implemented serial register plate across desktop and mobile, then run npm run validate.
